tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated serum creatinine), hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria with ketonuria and glucosuria, suggesting possible renal dysfunction or diabetic changes, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has abnormal odor, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation for renal pathology and review of glycemic status, considering possible analytical or sampling issues with HbA1c.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
